scenarios.title: The Growth Trap
scenarios.description: Fulfillment errors are at 3%. A new marketing campaign is ready to launch. Do you fix the foundation or chase the revenue?
scenarios.context: You are the Operations Lead. Error rates are rising, and the brand is taking a hit in reviews. Engineering says they need 4 weeks to automate fulfillment. Marketing wants to launch a huge campaign today.

scenarios.title: The Triage Tangle
scenarios.description: Internal requests are taking 9 days to process. Is the answer a better dashboard, or a harder conversation with Sales?
scenarios.context: Turnaround time is lagging. Sales is submitting messy data, but Leadership just wants a dashboard to 'see' the problem. You only have resources for one project.

scenarios.title: The Hiring Huddle
scenarios.description: The team is underwater. Do you hire more people to tread water, or stop to build the automation that saves them?
scenarios.context: Your team is working 60-hour weeks. You have budget for one new hire, OR you can spend that money on a software license that automates 40% of the work, but takes 3 months to set up.

scenarios.title: The Definition Duel
scenarios.description: Finance and Operations have different versions of 'Profit.' Who do you align with, and how do you break the news?
scenarios.context: The Board meeting is in two days. Finance says profit is X, you say it's Y because of how you calculate churn. You need a unified front without looking disorganized.

scenarios.title: The Project Pivot
scenarios.description: A high-priority project just lost its executive sponsor. Do you push forward in the dark or halt everything?
scenarios.context: The VP who championed your automation project just left the company. The rest of the leadership team is skeptical. You have a kickoff meeting in one hour.

scenarios.title: The Culture Shift
scenarios.description: A reorg has merged two teams with completely different cultures. How do you set the new standard?
scenarios.context: Team A is 'move fast and break things.' Team B is 'perfection or nothing.' You are now managing both, and they are clashing on every single process.

scenarios.title: The Metric Menace
scenarios.description: The team is hitting their 'volume' targets, but customer satisfaction is plummeting. How do you fix the incentive?
scenarios.context: Your current dashboard tracks 'Tickets Closed.' The team is closing easy tickets and ignoring hard ones. You need to propose a structural change to how performance is measured.
